name: Masala Khichdi
summary: A one-pot comfort dish of rice and split lentils (dal) simmered with turmeric, cumin, and ghee until soft and porridge-like.
steps: Rinse the rice and dal together.
Temper cumin in ghee, add turmeric, then the rice and dal.
Add water and salt, then simmer covered until soft and porridge-like.

name: Tadka Chawal
summary: Leftover rice revived with a tempering (tadka) of mustard seeds, onion, green chili, and turmeric — a quick way to use up cooked rice.
steps: Heat oil and crackle the mustard seeds.
Add onion, green chili, and turmeric; cook until soft.
Fold in the leftover rice and peas, season, and warm through.

name: Dal Chawal
summary: Everyday yellow dal, spiced with turmeric and a cumin-ghee tempering, served over steamed rice — simple, complete, and comforting.
steps: Cook the dal with turmeric and salt until soft.
Temper cumin in ghee and pour it over the dal.
Serve hot over steamed rice.

name: Sev Tamatar ki Sabzi
summary: A tangy tomato curry simmered with mustard seeds and chili, finished with crunchy sev (chickpea noodles) on top.
steps: Crackle mustard seeds in oil, add green chili and onion.
Add chopped tomato, turmeric, and salt; simmer into a thick gravy.
Top generously with sev just before serving.

name: Aloo ki Sabzi
summary: A simple everyday potato curry: cubed potatoes cooked with mustard seeds, cumin, and turmeric in a light masala.
steps: Temper mustard and cumin seeds in oil.
Add cubed potato, turmeric, and salt; stir to coat.
Add a little water and tomato, cover, and cook until tender.

name: Anda Bhurji
summary: Indian-style scrambled eggs (anda) cooked with onion, tomato, green chili, and turmeric — fast, spicy, and great with roti or bread.
steps: Fry onion, green chili, and tomato until soft.
Add turmeric, then pour in beaten eggs.
Scramble until set, season, and serve with roti or bread.

name: Dahi Chawal
summary: Soft rice mixed with yogurt (dahi) and a light mustard-seed tempering — cooling, soothing, and ready in minutes.
steps: Mash the cooked rice slightly and stir in yogurt and salt.
Crackle mustard seeds and curry leaves in oil.
Pour the tempering over the rice and mix.

name: Aloo Paratha
summary: Frozen paratha crisped on a pan and served with a spiced potato (aloo) side and a spoon of yogurt — an easy ready-to-cook plate.
steps: Cook the frozen paratha on a hot pan until golden on both sides.
Warm or lightly spice the potato as a side.
Serve with a spoon of yogurt.

name: Masala Maggi
summary: Instant noodles leveled up with sauteed onion, tomato, and a little turmeric — the desi hostel-room classic.
steps: Saute onion and tomato in a little oil.
Add water, the noodles, and the seasoning packet.
Cook until the noodles soak up the masala, then serve.

name: Paneer Bhurji
summary: Crumbled paneer (Indian cottage cheese) cooked with onion, tomato, chili, and turmeric — a quick high-protein vegetarian dish.
steps: Fry onion, green chili, and tomato until soft.
Add turmeric and crumbled paneer.
Toss until heated through, season, and serve with roti.

name: Veg Pulao
summary: Fragrant rice cooked with cumin, garam masala, peas, and mixed vegetables — a mildly spiced one-pot meal.
steps: Saute cumin and onion in oil.
Add the vegetables and garam masala, then the rinsed rice.
Add water and salt, cover, and cook until fluffy.

name: Dal Tadka
summary: Cooked dal with tomato and onion, finished with a sizzling tadka of cumin and garlic in ghee — restaurant-style comfort food.
steps: Cook dal with turmeric, tomato, and salt until soft.
In ghee, sizzle cumin and garlic until golden.
Pour the tempering over the dal and serve over rice.

name: Masala Dosa
summary: A crisp dosa made from fermented batter, filled with a soft spiced potato masala tempered with mustard seeds and turmeric.
steps: Make a spiced potato filling with mustard seeds, turmeric, and onion.
Spread the batter thin on a hot pan and drizzle with oil.
Add the filling, fold, and serve crisp.

name: Veg Frankie
summary: A Mumbai street-food wrap: spiced mashed potato rolled in a soft tortilla or roti with onion and a little yogurt.
steps: Mash and lightly spice the potato with turmeric and salt.
Warm the tortilla and spread the potato down the middle.
Add onion and a little yogurt, then roll up tightly.

name: Bombay Cheese Toast
summary: A spiced, cheesy grilled toast with onion and green chili — the iconic Bombay-style snack made on a buttered pan.
steps: Mix grated cheese with finely chopped onion, chili, and salt.
Spread on the tortilla or bread.
Toast on a buttered pan until crisp and melty.

name: Dal Palak
summary: Comforting dal cooked with spinach (palak) and a cumin-garlic tempering — protein and greens in one bowl.
steps: Cook dal with turmeric and salt until soft.
Add chopped spinach and tomato; simmer a few minutes.
Finish with a cumin-garlic tempering.

name: Aloo Tamatar ki Sabzi
summary: A quick everyday curry of potato (aloo) in a light tomato-onion gravy spiced with cumin and turmeric.
steps: Temper cumin, then add onion and tomato.
Add cubed potato, turmeric, and salt with a little water.
Cover and cook until the potato is soft.

name: Chicken Fried Rice
summary: Day-old rice stir-fried with chicken, egg, and onion in a little soy sauce — fast and satisfying.
steps: Scramble the egg in oil and set aside.
Stir-fry onion and chicken, then add the rice.
Toss with soy sauce and the egg until hot.

name: Chicken Quesadilla
summary: A crisp tortilla folded over melted cheese and chicken, cooked until golden — quick comfort food, great with salsa.
steps: Lay a tortilla in a lightly oiled pan.
Cover half with cheese and chicken, then fold.
Cook until golden and melty; serve with salsa.

name: Cheesy Scrambled Eggs
summary: Soft scrambled eggs cooked low and slow in butter, folded with melting cheese just before serving.
steps: Melt butter in a pan over low heat.
Add beaten eggs and stir gently as they set.
Fold in cheese, season, and serve.

name: Tortilla Pizza
summary: A crispy tortilla base topped with tomato (or salsa), cheese, and spinach, cooked until the cheese melts.
steps: Spread salsa or sliced tomato over the tortilla.
Add cheese and any toppings.
Cook until the base is crisp and the cheese melts.

name: Pantry Tomato Pasta
summary: Pasta tossed in a quick tomato-onion-garlic sauce, finished with cheese — built entirely from pantry basics.
steps: Boil the pasta until tender.
Soften onion and garlic, add tomato, and simmer into a sauce.
Toss the pasta through and top with cheese.

name: Loaded Baked Potato
summary: A soft baked potato split open and loaded with cheese and a spoon of yogurt.
steps: Bake or microwave the potato until tender.
Split it open and fluff the inside.
Top with cheese and a spoon of yogurt; season.

name: Besan Chilla
summary: A savory pancake made from gram flour (besan) batter with onion and tomato — high in protein and quick to cook.
steps: Whisk besan with water, turmeric, salt, onion, and chili into a smooth batter.
Pour a ladle onto a hot oiled pan and spread thin.
Cook until golden on both sides and serve hot with chutney.

name: Khaman Dhokla
summary: Soft, spongy steamed cakes made from gram flour (besan) and yogurt, finished with a mustard-seed tempering — a classic Gujarati snack.
steps: Mix besan with yogurt, salt, and a little water into a smooth batter; let it rest.
Pour into a greased tray and steam until spongy and set.
Temper mustard seeds and chili in oil, pour over the top, and cut into squares.

name: Rava Upma
summary: Roasted semolina (rava) cooked with onion, peas, and a mustard-curry leaf tempering into a soft, savory breakfast.
steps: Dry-roast the rava until lightly golden, then set aside.
Temper mustard seeds and curry leaves in oil; add onion and peas.
Add water, bring to a boil, stir in the rava, and cook until thick and fluffy.

name: Kanda Poha
summary: Flattened rice (poha) tossed with onion, turmeric, and a mustard tempering — a light, quick Maharashtrian breakfast.
steps: Rinse the poha briefly until just soft, then drain.
Temper mustard seeds and curry leaves in oil; add onion and turmeric.
Fold in the poha gently, season, and warm through.

name: Rava Dosa
summary: A thin, lacy, crisp crepe made from a runny semolina (rava) batter — no fermentation needed.
steps: Mix rava with water into a thin, runny batter with cumin, chili, and salt.
Pour from the edges of a hot pan inward to form a lacy crepe.
Drizzle oil and cook until crisp, then fold and serve.

name: Kadhi Chawal
summary: A tangy yogurt and gram-flour (besan) curry, gently spiced and served over steamed rice.
steps: Whisk besan and yogurt with water and turmeric until smooth and lump-free.
Simmer gently, stirring, until it thickens slightly.
Finish with a cumin-curry leaf tempering and serve over rice.

name: Dhokla
summary: Steamed savory cake, easy to make in a steamer or pressure cooker without whistle.
steps: Prep your ingredients: besan, yogurt.
Steamed savory cake, easy to make in a steamer or pressure cooker without whistle.
Serve hot.

name: Khandvi
summary: Thin rolled gram-flour snack; needs quick spreading while hot.
steps: Prep your ingredients: besan, yogurt, coconut.
Thin rolled gram-flour snack; needs quick spreading while hot.
Serve hot.

name: Thepla
summary: Travel-friendly flatbread usually served with pickle or yogurt.
steps: Prep your ingredients: whole wheat flour, methi leaves, yogurt.
Travel-friendly flatbread usually served with pickle or yogurt.
Serve hot.

name: Undhiyu
summary: Mixed vegetable curry; home version can use available vegetables.
steps: Prep your ingredients: mixed winter vegetables, methi muthia, coconut, peanuts.
Mixed vegetable curry; home version can use available vegetables.
Serve hot with rice or roti.

name: Sev Tameta nu Shaak
summary: Quick sweet-spicy tomato curry topped with sev.
steps: Prep your ingredients: tomatoes, sev.
Quick sweet-spicy tomato curry topped with sev.
Serve hot with rice or roti.

name: Gujarati Kadhi
summary: Sweet-tangy yogurt curry served with rice or khichdi.
steps: Prep your ingredients: yogurt, besan.
Sweet-tangy yogurt curry served with rice or khichdi.
Serve hot.

name: Handvo
summary: Savory baked or pan-cooked lentil cake.
steps: Prep your ingredients: rice-lentil batter, bottle gourd, sesame.
Savory baked or pan-cooked lentil cake.
Serve hot.

name: Fafda with Kadhi or Chutney
summary: Crispy gram-flour snack; deep-fried but simple ingredients.
steps: Prep your ingredients: besan.
Crispy gram-flour snack; deep-fried but simple ingredients.
Serve hot.

name: Poha
summary: Common breakfast dish; cooks quickly.
steps: Prep your ingredients: flattened rice, onion, peanuts.
Common breakfast dish; cooks quickly.
Serve hot.

name: Vada Pav
summary: Mumbai-style potato fritter sandwich.
steps: Prep your ingredients: potato, besan, pav.
Mumbai-style potato fritter sandwich.
Serve hot.

name: Misal Pav
summary: Can adjust spice level; serve with onion and lemon.
steps: Prep your ingredients: sprouted moth beans, farsan, pav, spicy gravy.
Can adjust spice level; serve with onion and lemon.
Serve hot.

name: Pithla Bhakri
summary: Rustic gram-flour curry with millet flatbread.
steps: Prep your ingredients: besan, jowar.
Rustic gram-flour curry with millet flatbread.
Serve hot.

name: Sabudana Khichdi
summary: Popular fasting dish; soak sabudana properly.
steps: Prep your ingredients: tapioca pearls, peanuts, potato.
Popular fasting dish; soak sabudana properly.
Serve hot.

name: Bharli Vangi
summary: Stuffed brinjal curry; pairs well with bhakri or rice.
steps: Prep your ingredients: small eggplants, peanuts, coconut.
Stuffed brinjal curry; pairs well with bhakri or rice.
Serve hot.

name: Kolhapuri Chicken
summary: Spicy chicken curry; reduce chilies for a milder version.
steps: Prep your ingredients: chicken, dry coconut.
Spicy chicken curry; reduce chilies for a milder version.
Serve hot with rice or roti.

name: Malvani Fish Curry
summary: Coastal curry usually served with rice.
steps: Prep your ingredients: fish, coconut.
Coastal curry usually served with rice.
Serve hot.

name: Dal Baati Churma
summary: Classic meal of baked baati, dal, and sweet churma.
steps: Prep your ingredients: wheat flour, mixed dal.
Classic meal of baked baati, dal, and sweet churma.
Serve hot.

name: Gatte ki Sabzi
summary: Gram-flour dumplings cooked in yogurt gravy.
steps: Prep your ingredients: besan, yogurt.
Gram-flour dumplings cooked in yogurt gravy.
Serve hot with rice or roti.

name: Ker Sangri
summary: Traditional dry curry; use dried ker sangri if available.
steps: Prep your ingredients: ker berries, sangri beans.
Traditional dry curry; use dried ker sangri if available.
Serve hot with rice or roti.

name: Papad ki Sabzi
summary: Quick curry when fresh vegetables are limited.
steps: Prep your ingredients: papad, yogurt.
Quick curry when fresh vegetables are limited.
Serve hot with rice or roti.

name: Bajre ki Roti with Lehsun Chutney
summary: Hearty millet roti served with spicy chutney.
steps: Prep your ingredients: bajra flour.
Hearty millet roti served with spicy chutney.
Serve hot.

name: Rajasthani Kadhi
summary: Tangier and spicier than Gujarati kadhi.
steps: Prep your ingredients: yogurt, besan.
Tangier and spicier than Gujarati kadhi.
Serve hot with rice or roti.

name: Laal Maas
summary: Fiery meat curry; home version can use Kashmiri chili for color with less heat.
steps: Prep your ingredients: mutton, yogurt.
Fiery meat curry; home version can use Kashmiri chili for color with less heat.
Serve hot with rice or roti.

name: Safed Maas
summary: Rich white meat curry for special meals.
steps: Prep your ingredients: mutton, yogurt, cream, cashew.
Rich white meat curry for special meals.
Serve hot with rice or roti.

name: Rajma Chawal
summary: Common Punjabi-style kidney bean curry with rice.
steps: Prep your ingredients: kidney beans, onion, tomato, rice.
Common Punjabi-style kidney bean curry with rice.
Serve hot.

name: Chole Bhature
summary: Chole can be made at home; bhature are deep-fried breads.
steps: Prep your ingredients: chickpeas, tea, flour.
Chole can be made at home; bhature are deep-fried breads.
Serve hot.

name: Dal Makhani
summary: Slow-cooked creamy lentils; pressure cooker speeds it up.
steps: Prep your ingredients: whole urad dal, rajma, butter, cream.
Slow-cooked creamy lentils; pressure cooker speeds it up.
Serve hot with rice or roti.

name: Paneer Butter Masala
summary: Restaurant-style curry that is easy to make at home.
steps: Prep your ingredients: paneer, tomato, butter, cream.
Restaurant-style curry that is easy to make at home.
Serve hot with rice or roti.

name: Kadhi Pakora
summary: North Indian yogurt curry with fritters.
steps: Prep your ingredients: yogurt, besan, onion pakoras.
North Indian yogurt curry with fritters.

name: Butter Chicken
summary: Creamy chicken curry; use boneless or bone-in chicken.
steps: Prep your ingredients: chicken, tomato, butter, cream.
Creamy chicken curry; use boneless or bone-in chicken.

name: Chicken Biryani
summary: Layered rice dish; can be made dum-style in a heavy pot.
steps: Prep your ingredients: chicken, basmati rice, yogurt, fried onions.
Layered rice dish; can be made dum-style in a heavy pot.

name: Rogan Josh
summary: Kashmiri-style aromatic meat curry.
steps: Prep your ingredients: lamb, yogurt.

name: Idli
summary: Steamed fermented rice-lentil cakes.
steps: Prep your ingredients: rice, urad dal.
Steamed fermented rice-lentil cakes.

name: Dosa
summary: Crisp fermented crepes served with chutney and sambar.
steps: Prep your ingredients: rice, urad dal.
Crisp fermented crepes served with chutney and sambar.

name: Sambar
summary: Lentil-vegetable stew common across South India.
steps: Prep your ingredients: toor dal, vegetables, sambar powder.
Lentil-vegetable stew common across South India.

name: Rasam
summary: Thin spicy-sour soup served with rice.
steps: Prep your ingredients: tomato, dal water.
Thin spicy-sour soup served with rice.

steps: Prep your ingredients: semolina, onion, vegetables.

steps: Prep your ingredients: mixed vegetables, coconut, yogurt.

name: Hyderabadi Chicken Biryani
summary: Aromatic dum biryani; can be made in a thick-bottomed pot.
steps: Prep your ingredients: chicken, basmati rice, yogurt, mint.

summary: Spicy Tamil-style chicken curry with roasted spices.
steps: Prep your ingredients: chicken, coconut.
Spicy Tamil-style chicken curry with roasted spices.
Serve hot with rice or roti.

name: Kerala Fish Curry
summary: Tangy coastal curry served with rice.
steps: Prep your ingredients: fish, coconut milk.
Tangy coastal curry served with rice.

name: Egg Curry South Indian Style
summary: Simple egg curry with South Indian tempering.
steps: Prep your ingredients: onion, tomato, coconut.
Simple egg curry with South Indian tempering.

name: Assamese Khar
summary: Traditional Assamese alkaline dish; use food-safe khar substitute if needed.
steps: Prep your ingredients: raw papaya, lentils.
Traditional Assamese alkaline dish; use food-safe khar substitute if needed.
Serve hot with rice or roti.

name: Aloo Pitika
summary: Assamese mashed potato side dish.
steps: Prep your ingredients: potato, onion.
Assamese mashed potato side dish.
Serve hot with rice or roti.

name: Black Rice Kheer
summary: Manipuri-inspired dessert using black rice.
steps: Prep your ingredients: black rice, milk.
Manipuri-inspired dessert using black rice.

name: Manipuri Eromba
summary: Traditionally may include fermented fish; this version can be vegetarian.
steps: Prep your ingredients: fermented bamboo shoot, herbs.
Traditionally may include fermented fish; this version can be vegetarian.
Serve hot with rice or roti.

name: Mizo Bai
summary: Light boiled vegetable dish; can be made with local greens.
steps: Prep your ingredients: greens, vegetables, bamboo shoot, simple seasoning.
Light boiled vegetable dish; can be made with local greens.
Serve hot with rice or roti.

name: Assamese Masor Tenga
summary: Light sour fish curry served with rice.
steps: Prep your ingredients: fish, tomato, lemon.
Light sour fish curry served with rice.

name: Naga Pork Curry with Bamboo Shoot
summary: Spicy pork curry; adjust chili carefully.
steps: Prep your ingredients: pork, fermented bamboo shoot.
Spicy pork curry; adjust chili carefully.

name: Smoked Pork with Axone
summary: Nagaland-style dish; use axone only if available and ventilate well while cooking.
steps: Prep your ingredients: smoked pork, axone fermented soybean.
Nagaland-style dish; use axone only if available and ventilate well while cooking.
Serve hot.

name: Chicken with Bamboo Shoot
summary: Simple North East-style chicken curry with minimal spices.
steps: Prep your ingredients: chicken, bamboo shoot.
Simple North East-style chicken curry with minimal spices.

summary: Can be grilled or served cold.
